{{日本の古墳| 名称=白石古墳群| 所在地=群馬県藤岡市| 主要古墳=七輿山古墳 白い石稲荷山古墳| }} 白石古墳群は、藤岡市白石などに分布する古墳群。
==概要== 白石古墳群は、藤岡市白石、上落合などに分布する古墳群で、七輿山支群、稲荷山支群、猿田支群、下郷支群の4つで構成される。古墳群の始まりは、鮎川の段丘上に築かれた白石稲荷山古墳でそこから巨大古墳や群集墳が築かれていった。詳細は各支群にて記載。 ==七輿山支群== *七輿山古墳 *伊勢塚古墳 *宗永寺東裏塚古墳 *宗永寺西裏塚古墳 *平井地区1号墳 *皇子塚古墳 *薬師塚古墳 ==猿田支群== *猿田古墳 *鍋塚古墳 *大御堂古墳 *廣瀬古墳 *城山古墳 ==稲荷山支群== *白石稲荷山古墳 *十二天塚古墳 *十二天塚北古墳 *新堀大塚古墳 ==下郷支群== *滝1号墳 *江原塚古墳 *堀越塚古墳 *白石二子山古墳 *萩原塚古墳 *佐平塚古墳

{{日本の古墳| 名称=白石古墳群| 所在地=群馬県藤岡市| 主要古墳=七輿山古墳 白い石稲荷山古墳| }} 白石古墳群は、藤岡市白石などに分布する古墳群。

==猿田支群== *猿田古墳 *鍋塚古墳 *大御堂古墳 *廣瀬古墳 *城山古墳 ==稲荷山支群== *白石稲荷山古墳 *十二天塚古墳 *十二天塚北古墳 *新堀大塚古墳 ==下郷支群== *滝1号墳 *江原塚古墳 *堀越塚古墳 *白石二子山古墳 *萩原塚古墳 *佐平塚古墳

{{日本の古墳 |名称=白石古墳群 |所在地=群馬県藤岡市 |主要古墳=七輿山古墳、白石稲荷山古墳 |}} '''白石古墳群'''（しろいしこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]白石などに分布する[[古墳群]]。

==七輿山支群== *[[七輿山古墳]] *[[伊勢塚古墳]] *宗永寺東裏塚古墳 *宗永寺西裏塚古墳 *[[平井地区1号墳]] *[[皇子塚古墳]] *薬師塚古墳
==猿田支群== *猿田古墳 *鍋塚古墳 *大御堂古墳 *廣瀬古墳 *城山古墳

revision.text:
{{Infobox burial site | name = Shiroishi Kofun Group | image = [[File:Nanakoshiyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳}} ({{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | built = 5th to late 6th century | designation = {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳}} (National Historic Site) }}
'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (Kofuns) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>
== Overview == The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|ja|白石稲荷山古墳}} on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳}} * {{ill|Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|ja|白石稲荷山古墳}} * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群}}

{{Infobox burial site | name = Shiroishi Kofun Group | image = [[File:Nanakoshiyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳}} ({{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真}}) {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}} | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | built = 5th to late 6th century | designation = {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳}} (National Historic Site) }}
'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (Kofuns) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>
== Overview == The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|ja|白石稲荷山古墳}} on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳}} * {{ill|Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|ja|白石稲荷山古墳}} * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群}}

== Inariyama Group == * {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|ja|白石稲荷山古墳|12=draft}} * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiroishi Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiroishi Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiroishi Kofun Group]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiroishi Kofun Group]] [[ja:白石古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox burial site | name = Shiroishi Kofun Group | image = [[File:Nanakoshiyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}) {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}} | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | built = 5th to late 6th century | designation = {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳|12=draft}} (National Historic Site) }}
'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (Kofuns) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>
== Overview == The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|ja|白石稲荷山古墳|12=draft}} on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|ja|七輿山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=draft}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|ja|白石稲荷山古墳|12=draft}} * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiroishi Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiroishi Kofun Group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiroishi Kofun Group]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shiroishi Kofun Group]] [[ja:白石古墳群]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白石古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Fujioka|WD=Q596486}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q85882562]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox burial site | name = Shiroishi Kofun Group | image = [[File:Nanakoshiyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] ({{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | built = 5th to late 6th century | designation = [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] (National Historic Site) }}
'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofuns) located in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>
== Overview == The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/images of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白石古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Fujioka|WD=Q596486}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q85882562]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox burial site | name = Shiroishi Kofun Group | image = [[File:Nanakoshiyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] ({{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | built = 5th to late 6th century | designation = [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] (National Historic Site) }}
'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofuns) located in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>
== Overview == The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/images of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}}

It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/images of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}}

It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白石古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白石古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白石古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Fujioka|WD=Q596486}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q85882562]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox burial site | name = Shiroishi Kofun Group | image = [[File:Nanakoshiyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] ({{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | built = 5th to late 6th century | designation = [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] (National Historic Site) }}
'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofuns) located in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>
== Overview == The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/images of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白石古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白石古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白石古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Fujioka|WD=Q596486}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85882562]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox burial site | name = Shiroishi Kofun Group | image = [[File:Nanakoshiyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]] | image_caption = [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] ({{ill|National Aerial Photography|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Aerial Photography|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}) {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{coord|36|15|15.5|N|139|02|24.2|E|region:JP-10|display=inline,title}} | built = 5th to late 6th century | designation = [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] (National Historic Site) }}
'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofuns) located in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>
== Overview == The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/images of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}}

'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (Kofuns) located in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka, Gunma]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>
== Overview == The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.
== Nanakoshiyama Group == * [[:en:Nanakoshiyama Kofun|Nanakoshiyama Kofun]] * {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun * Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun * Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun * {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}} * Yakushizuka Kofun
== Saruta Group == * Saruta Kofun * Nabetsuka Kofun * Ōmido Kofun * Hirose Kofun * Shiroyama Kofun
== Inariyama Group == * [[:en:Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun]] * Jūnitendzuka Kofun * Jūnitendzuka North Kofun * Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun
== Shimogo Group == * Taki No. 1 Kofun * Ebarazuka Kofun * Horikoshizuka Kofun * Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun * Hagiwarazuka Kofun * Sabei-zuka Kofun
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}} * {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|images of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳画像一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/images of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja|毛野国白石丘陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}}

'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (Kofuns) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>

== Overview ==
The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|en|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q17216721|lt=Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.

== Nanakoshiyama Group ==
* {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|en|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|WD=Q11353699|lt=Nanakoshiyama Kofun}}
* {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}}
* Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun
* Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun
* Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun
* {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}}
* Yakushizuka Kofun

== Saruta Group ==
* Saruta Kofun
* Nabetsuka Kofun
* Ōmido Kofun
* Hirose Kofun
* Shiroyama Kofun

== Inariyama Group ==
* {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|en|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q17216721|lt=Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}}
* Jūnitendzuka Kofun
* Jūnitendzuka North Kofun
* Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun

== Shimogo Group ==
* Taki No. 1 Kofun
* Ebarazuka Kofun
* Horikoshizuka Kofun
* Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun
* Hagiwarazuka Kofun
* Sabei-zuka Kofun

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}
* {{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture Board of Education|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/03/x4500021.html|title=The Kofun Period in Gunma: Center of Eastern Japan Culture|accessdate=2023-01-14}}

'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (Kofuns) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>

== Overview ==
The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|en|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q17216721|lt=Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.

== Nanakoshiyama Group ==
* {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|en|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|WD=Q11353699|lt=Nanakoshiyama Kofun}}
* {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}}
* Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun
* Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun
* Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun
* {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}}
* Yakushizuka Kofun

== Saruta Group ==
* Saruta Kofun
* Nabetsuka Kofun
* Ōmido Kofun
* Hirose Kofun
* Shiroyama Kofun

== Inariyama Group ==
* {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|en|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q17216721|lt=Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}}
* Jūnitendzuka Kofun
* Jūnitendzuka North Kofun
* Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun

== Shimogo Group ==
* Taki No. 1 Kofun
* Ebarazuka Kofun
* Horikoshizuka Kofun
* Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun
* Hagiwarazuka Kofun
* Sabei-zuka Kofun

{{Reflist}}

'''Shiroishi Kofun Group''' (白石古墳群) is a group of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (Kofuns) located in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka, Gunma|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It spans the 5th to late 6th centuries and is designated as part of the "Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park" (毛野国白石丘陵公園).<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=Gunma Prefecture|url=https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/kenokuni0.html|title=Keno-no-Kuni Shiroishi Hills Park|accessdate=2023-01-14}}</ref>

== Overview ==
The Shiroishi Kofun Group comprises Kofuns in the Shiroishi, Kami-Ochiai, and other areas of Fujioka. It is divided into four subgroups: the Nanakoshiyama, Inariyama, Saruta, and Shimogo groups.<ref>{{Cite journal|和書|last=Shimura|first=Tetsu|title=Chapter 2: Geographical and Historical Environment of the Ruins (Part 2: Excavation of the Saruta II Site)|journal=Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=120|date=2004-03-31|DOI=10.15024/00001312}}</ref> The construction of the group began with the {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|en|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q17216721|lt=Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|1=Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}} on the Ayukawa terrace, followed by the creation of massive tombs and cluster tombs. Detailed descriptions of each subgroup are provided below.

== Nanakoshiyama Group ==
* {{ill|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|en|Nanakoshiyama Kofun|WD=Q11353699|lt=Nanakoshiyama Kofun|1=Nanakoshiyama Kofun}}
* {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isetsuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}}
* Sōeiji Higashi-Uratsuka Kofun
* Sōeiji Nishi-Uratsuka Kofun
* Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun
* {{ill|Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|ja|皇子塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-zuka Kofun (Fujioka)|qq=}}
* Yakushizuka Kofun

== Saruta Group ==
* Saruta Kofun
* Nabetsuka Kofun
* Ōmido Kofun
* Hirose Kofun
* Shiroyama Kofun

== Inariyama Group ==
* {{ill|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|en|Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|WD=Q17216721|lt=Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun|1=Shiroishi Inariyama Kofun}}
* Jūnitendzuka Kofun
* Jūnitendzuka North Kofun
* Shinbori Ōtsuka Kofun

== Shimogo Group ==
* Taki No. 1 Kofun
* Ebarazuka Kofun
* Horikoshizuka Kofun
* Shiroishi Futagoyama Kofun
* Hagiwarazuka Kofun
* Sabei-zuka Kofun
